user_message: Need help with Fourth Coffee -- draft the follow-up. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 16:06 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 13:53 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 13:35 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-02-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is whitespace analysis.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:10:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-I trap (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-I failure mode (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-I -- Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context
